---
title: "Assign cells of EEA grid to European countries"
author: 
  - Soria Delva
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    number_sections: true
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Goal

Given the EEA reference grid of Europe at 1km resolution, this document assigns cells to their respective countries.

# Setup 

```{r load_libraries, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse) 
library(tidylog) 
library(here) 
library(sf) 
library(mapview)
```

# Read input data

## Occurrence data

We read the occurrence cube of the PrIUS II taxa generated by script 1_download_custom_cubes.Rmd.
This was generated on 23/02/2026.

NOTE: the species cube is (too) heavy to push to the git. This will give an error when running the script. I needed to download it locally (making connection with zenodo within the script was too slow). So maybe best to download the most recent version of the species cube and put it in the folder data/input before running it.

```{r update cube locally}
fistools::download_gdrive_if_missing(gfileID = "154S4vww2G1f0vH7_jt1cZJXeUEmXlZaL",
                                     destfile = "prius2/input/prius2_species_cube.csv",
                                     update_always = TRUE,
                                     email = Sys.getenv("email"))
```

```{r read_data_cube, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
species_cube <- read_csv(
  file =here::here("prius2", "input", "prius2_species_cube.csv"),
  col_types = cols(
    year = col_double(),
    eeacellcode = col_character(),
    specieskey = col_double(),
    occurrences = col_double(),
    mincoordinateuncertaintyinmeters = col_double()
  ),na = "")%>%
 tidylog::rename(eea_cell_code = eeacellcode,
         taxonKey = specieskey,
         obs = occurrences) %>%
 tidylog::filter(!is.na(eea_cell_code)) #Remove data not assigned to any cell code

gc()
```


## Load EEA grid of countries relevant for buffer


```{r Load_country_EEA_grid}
#Check different layers of the EEA grid geopackage
EEA_grid_Europe <- file.path("prius2","spatial", "EEA-1km-GRID-COUNTRIES-2013.gpkg")
layers_info <- st_layers(EEA_grid_Europe)

#Create a dataset for each region of interest
France <- st_read(EEA_grid_Europe, layer = "fr_1km_polygon")
UK <- st_read(EEA_grid_Europe, layer = "gb_1km_polygon")
Germany <- st_read(EEA_grid_Europe, layer = "de_1km_polygon")
Netherlands <- st_read(EEA_grid_Europe, layer = "nl_1km_polygon")
Luxembourg <- st_read(EEA_grid_Europe, layer = "lu_1km_polygon")
Switzerland <- st_read(EEA_grid_Europe, layer = "ch_1km_polygon")
```


```{r Process_country_eeagrids}
#The country EEA grids also include cells far over the border of neighbouring countries
#Filter those out using the EU + EEZ shapefile as overlay: here we will prepare these files
europe_eez<-st_read(file.path("prius2", "spatial", "EEZ_land_union_v4_202410.shp"))

#Check the eez file visually
ggplot()+
geom_sf(data=europe_eez, aes(fill=UNION))+
theme_bw()+
theme(legend.position="none")

#Prepare land + eez shapefile for each country of interest
France_eez<-filter(europe_eez, UNION=="France")%>%
  st_transform(crs=st_crs(France))
UK_eez<-filter(europe_eez, UNION=="United Kingdom")%>%
  st_transform(crs=st_crs(France))
Netherlands_eez<-filter(europe_eez, UNION=="Netherlands")%>%
  st_transform(crs=st_crs(France))
Germany_eez<-filter(europe_eez, UNION=="Germany")%>%
  st_transform(crs=st_crs(France))
Switzerland_eez<-filter(europe_eez, UNION=="Switzerland")%>%
  st_transform(crs=st_crs(France))
Luxembourg_eez <- filter(europe_eez, UNION=="Grand Duchy of Luxembourg")%>%
  st_transform(crs=st_crs(France))

#Visual check
mapview::mapview(Luxembourg_eez)
```


```{r Filter_eea_cells}
# Define a function to convert MULTISURFACE to MULTIPOLYGON and filter based on EEZ shapefile
extract_country_cells <- function(country_grid, country_eez) {
  
  # Extract geometry
  geom <- st_geometry(country_grid)
  
  # Convert MULTISURFACE to MULTIPOLYGON
  geom_multipolygon <- st_sfc(lapply(geom, function(x) {
    st_cast(x, "MULTIPOLYGON")
  }), crs = st_crs(country_grid))
  
  # Rebuild sf object with new geometry
  country_grid_mp <- st_sf(country_grid[, setdiff(names(country_grid), "geom")],
                           geometry = geom_multipolygon)
  
  # Filter cells by EEZ overlap
  country_cells <- st_filter(country_grid_mp, country_eez)
  
  return(country_cells)
}

#Per country, we keep only cells within the country and its EEZ
France<-extract_country_cells(France, France_eez)
UK<-extract_country_cells(UK, UK_eez)
Netherlands<-extract_country_cells(Netherlands, Netherlands_eez)
Germany<-extract_country_cells(Germany, Germany_eez)
Switzerland<-extract_country_cells(Switzerland, Switzerland_eez)
Luxembourg<-extract_country_cells(Luxembourg, Luxembourg_eez)
mapview::mapview(Luxembourg)
gc()
```

```{r Load_cells_Wallonia_Brussels}
#Load Belgium eea grid cells
Belgium <- st_read(file.path("prius2", "spatial", "utm1_bel_with_regions.gpkg"))%>%
  select(CELLCODE, isWallonia, isBrussels)

Brussels <- filter(Belgium, isBrussels)
Wallonia <- filter(Belgium, isWallonia)
```


## Visualize to check

```{r Visualize country cells}
Europe<-st_read(file.path("prius2","spatial", "EUROPE.shp"))%>%
  st_transform(st_crs(France))

#Get buffer of 400km
vla <- st_read("./prius/data/spatial/flanders_wgs84.geojson")
vla_buf_wkt <- vla %>% 
  st_buffer(vla, dist = 400000) %>% 
  st_geometry() %>% 
  st_simplify()

ggplot()+
  geom_sf(data=Europe)+
  geom_sf(data=France%>%st_union, fill="red", alpha=0.5)+
  geom_sf(data=UK%>%st_union(), fill="blue", alpha=0.5)+
  geom_sf(data=Germany%>%st_union(), fill="green", alpha=0.5)+
  geom_sf(data=Netherlands%>%st_union(), fill="orange", alpha=0.5)+
  geom_sf(data=Luxembourg%>%st_union(), fill="yellow", alpha=0.5)+
  geom_sf(data=Switzerland%>%st_union(), fill="black", alpha=0.5)+
  geom_sf(data=Wallonia%>%st_union(), fill="purple", alpha=0.5)+
  geom_sf(data=Brussels%>%st_union(), fill="pink", alpha=0.5)+
  geom_sf(data = vla_buf_wkt, color="black", fill=NA, size=2)+
  theme_bw()

```


## Assign cells of speciescube to countries

```{r Assign_countries}

species_cube <- species_cube %>%
  tidylog::mutate(inFrance = eea_cell_code %in% France$CELLCODE,
                  inGermany = eea_cell_code %in% Germany$CELLCODE,
                  inNetherlands = eea_cell_code %in% Netherlands$CELLCODE,
                  inSwitzerland = eea_cell_code %in% Switzerland$CELLCODE,
                  inLuxembourg =  eea_cell_code %in% Luxembourg$CELLCODE,
                  inUK = eea_cell_code %in% UK$CELLCODE,
                  inBrussels = eea_cell_code %in% Brussels$CELLCODE,
                  inWallonia =  eea_cell_code %in% Wallonia$CELLCODE)
  
  
  #Final check
# cells_in_France <- species_cube %>%
#   filter(inFrance) %>%
#   pull(eea_cell_code)
# cells_in_Netherlands <- species_cube %>%
#   filter(inNetherlands) %>%
#   pull(eea_cell_code)
# 
# check_France<- France %>%
#   filter(CELLCODE %in% cells_in_France)
# check_Netherlands<- Netherlands %>%
#   filter(CELLCODE %in% cells_in_Netherlands)
# 
# st_write(check_France, "check_France.shp")
# st_write(check_Netherlands, "check_Netherlands.shp")
```

##Export species cube with region info
```{r Store be_grid}
write_csv(species_cube, here::here("prius2", "input", "speciescube_country_assignment.csv"))

```
